title: capsella bursa-pastoris
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris, also known as shepherd's purse, is a biennial or annual plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and heart-shaped seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of shepherd's purse include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate menstrual cramps, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Shepherd's purse can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
